title: The Jersey Beach That Lied About Its Presidents
description:
The name on the park sign promises seven American presidents once called this stretch of Jersey Shore home. The truth? The real number might be four. Maybe five. But the stories they left behind are far more extraordinary than any myth.

This documentary explores Long Branch, New Jersey -- once the most glamorous resort in America, richer than Newport, more daring than Saratoga Springs -- and the presidents who came here to rest, to gamble, to die, and to win elections. Grant built the nation's first Summer White House here. Garfield was carried here by volunteers who laid a railroad overnight by torchlight. Wilson ran his entire 1916 re-election campaign from a 52-room mansion five months before asking Congress to declare war.

This is Video 1 of the Seven Presidents series. Five videos. Seven presidents. This is just the beginning.

Chapters:
0:00 - The Myth: Hook
1:30 - America's Summer Capital
3:00 - Grant and the First Summer White House
4:30 - The Garfield Story
5:30 - The Disputed Presidents
6:10 - Wilson, McKinley, and the End of an Era
7:30 - What Endures

---

Video 5: When Long Branch Was America's Playground (Resort Era)
